task_description: Guide a small controlled vehicle to make a safe landing at the target zone. A good policy should approach the target center, descend gently, remain upright, use limited control effort, and finish with stable ground contact instead of bouncing or crashing.

environment_source.observation_space_description: The observation is an 8-dimensional vector. Index 0 is horizontal position relative to the target center. Index 1 is vertical position. Index 2 is horizontal velocity. Index 3 is vertical velocity. Index 4 is body tilt angle. Index 5 is angular velocity. Index 6 is whether the left support leg is touching the ground. Index 7 is whether the right support leg is touching the ground.
environment_source.action_space_description: The action is a discrete scalar control command. Action 0 applies no thrust. Action 1 applies side thrust in one direction. Action 2 applies the main upward thrust. Action 3 applies side thrust in the opposite direction.
environment_source.step_semantics_summary: The step function advances rigid-body dynamics, returns the next observation vector, an environment reward, termination flags, and auxiliary info. The environment reward must not be used as feedback for reward editing. It may be recorded only for posthoc reporting.
environment_source.termination_summary: Episodes may terminate after unsafe impact, out-of-bounds behavior, or successful stable contact, depending on the simulator state. The reward generator should prefer task-state evidence rather than relying on hidden simulator reward values.
